title: arachnorchis longicauda subsp. clivicola
intro: Arachnorchis longicauda subsp. clivicola, also known as the Rock Orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The Rock Orchid is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, due to its rich composition of bioactive compounds. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes are often dried and ground into a powder, then used to make infusions, teas, and topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
